column1: Exported from the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services (CMS) Medicaid and Children's Health Insurance Program (CHIP) Core Set Data Dashboard, https://www.medicaid.gov/medicaid/quality-of-care/core-set-data-dashboard/. If the user sorted these data on the website, the sorting will not carry over into this file.

Opening this file in Excel may alter the formatting of some values. In particular, Excel may add or remove trailing zeros in decimal places regardless of original formatting. Please open the file in a text editor or equivalent to see the values as they were exported.

column2: Prenatal and Postpartum Care: Age 21 and Older (PPC2-AD)

Percentage of live birth deliveries to women age 21 and older that had (1) a prenatal care visit in the first trimester, on or before the Medicaid or CHIP enrollment start date, or within 42 days of enrollment, and (2) a postpartum visit on or between 7 and 84 days after delivery.

column3: Population
column5: Percentage of Women Age 21 and Older Delivering a Live Birth who had a Postpartum Care Visit on or Between 7 and 84 Days after Delivery
column6: Note

column3: Medicaid (Title XIX), Title XXI-funded Medicaid Expansion CHIP
column6: State-specific comments: Rates include managed care population (12 MCOs). State reported that rates exclude the following populations: "Individuals that moved between programs, plans, and/or delivery systems. The FFS population is not included as the state does not collect the voluntary measures for this population." The state described the "other" data sources used to calculate the measure as the following: "Data submitted by the health plans." Rates were derived using both administrative and hybrid method data. 8 plans reported using the Hybrid Methodology. 4 plans reported using the Administrative Methodology. Rates were audited or validated.

column3: Medicaid (Title XIX)
column6: State-specific comments: Rates include managed care population (3 MCOs). State reported that rates exclude the following populations: "FFS Excluded." The state described the "other" data sources used to calculate the measure as the following: "The Managed Care Organization's data is from their claims system." Rates were audited or validated. State noted: "Rate includes data from NH's three managed care programs which account for 99% of NH's full benefit Medicaid enrollment. The State of NH Medicaid program saw a substantial drop in enrollment in calendar year 2023, due to the COVID Public Health Emergency ending 3/31/2023."

column3: Medicaid (Title XIX), Title XXI-funded Medicaid Expansion CHIP, Individuals Dually Eligible for Medicare and Medicaid, Other
column6: State-specific comments: Rates include FFS and managed care populations (16 MCOs). The state described the "other" population included in the measure as the following: "Separate CHIP." Rates were derived using both administrative and hybrid method data. Deliveries for the FFS population were calculated using administrative data (1,403 deliveries). Deliveries for the managed care population (16 plans) were reviewed using hybrid data (sampling 4,816 cases out of 11,526 total eligible deliveries).

column3: Medicaid (Title XIX), Title XXI-funded Medicaid Expansion CHIP
column6: State-specific comments: Rates include managed care population (11 MCOs). State reported that rates exclude the following populations: "Excludes the FFS population, which is inclusive of individuals that moved between programs, plans, or delivery systems." The state described the "other" data sources used to calculate the measure as the following: "The administrative data includes claims (including pharmacy and dental), eligibility, provider, and supplemental data from the MCOs." Rates were derived using both administrative and hybrid method data. The reporting entities are managed care organizations (MCO). Eight (8) MCOs used the hybrid data source and three (3) MCOs used the administrative data source. The administrative method was used to include measure-eligible enrollees who switched between Medicaid and CHIP programs, delivery systems, or MCOs. Rates were audited or validated.

The state described the "other" data sources used to calculate the measure as the following: "Administrative data as provided in HEDIS reports by Medicaid Managed Care Organizations (MCOs)." Rates were audited or validated.

column3: Medicaid (Title XIX), Title XXI-funded Medicaid Expansion CHIP
column6: State-specific comments: Rates include FFS and managed care populations (16 MCOs). The state described the "other" data sources used to calculate the measure as the following: "Two data sources were used to calculate the quality of care indicators: member level enrollment information and member level health care claims/encounter data. The enrollment files contained information about the member's age, gender, the MCO, and program in which the member is enrolled per month. Member-level claims/encounter data contained CPT, ICD10-CM, and place of service codes, and other information necessary to calculate the quality of care indicators." Rates were audited or validated.

column3: Medicaid (Title XIX)
column6: State-specific comments: Rates include managed care population (4 MCOs). The state described the "other" data sources used to calculate the measure as the following: "Rate includes Medicaid claims and encounter data. Prior year submissions only included encounter data. Reports generated using an administrative methodology. Rates may vary from the previous year that were calculated using a hybrid methodology." Rates were audited or validated.

column3: Medicaid (Title XIX), Individuals Dually Eligible for Medicare and Medicaid
column6: State-specific comments: Rates include FFS, PCCM, and managed care populations (5 MCOs). State reported that rates exclude the following populations: "WA excluded beneficiaries with third-party-liability (TPL). Of the Medicaid eligible population, 16% had TPL." The state described the "other" data sources used to calculate the measure as the following: "Vital records." State reported the following variation: "WA included paid fee-for-service claims and reported managed care encounters that met state's data quality standards regardless of payment status by the MCO, as well as denied fee-for-service claims with a denial reason specifying third party liability or other payers. Denied fee-for-service claims were only evaluated for deliveries, prenatal visits, and postpartum visits, but not for non-live indicators or hospice use." State noted: "WA used vital records data as a supplement to administrative MMIS data. A birth certificate was found for 97.8% of the eligible population. When determining enrollment, WA used the 15th of the month."
